channel.title: Microsoft on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Microsoft on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: security: Real world incident response: Microsoft and AXA XL strengthen cyber resilience
item.description:
Microsoft and AXA XL are collaborating to give cyber insurance policyholders direct access to Microsoft Incident Response services. The goal is to help organizations coordinate technical response, business decisions, and insurance actions during incidents.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding
item.description:
Microsoft Security published a post on applying least-privilege principles to AI agents, focusing on identity, access control, tool binding, and auditing as agents become more autonomous.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Defending SaaS-based applications against ShinyHunters OAuth abuse
item.description:
Microsoft Threat Intelligence reported ShinyHunters-linked activity targeting SaaS-based applications through OAuth abuse, vishing, supply-chain compromise, and misconfigured guest access. The post focuses on how these techniques were used against SaaS environments and how to defend against them.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Securing our future: July 2026 progress report on Microsoft’s Secure Future Initiative
item.description:
Microsoft published its July 2026 Secure Future Initiative progress report, summarizing work on secure foundations, AI-assisted defense, and broader cybersecurity readiness. The provided text is only a brief overview and does not include specific implementation details or metrics.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Protecting Microsoft at AI speed: How SFI proactively hardens our cloud
item.description:
Microsoft says its Secure Future Initiative (SFI) uses security requirements, threat intelligence, and operational frameworks to define what a well-defended cloud service should look like. It emphasizes continuously evaluating live services against those requirements at AI speed.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: commandline: How my dev machine built 1,111 word-processing features and forgot to add a ruler
item.description:
The piece describes an experiment where a complex agentic AI loop was used to build an online collaborative word processor, reflecting on the results and what emerged from letting the system run mostly on its own. The title suggests the system produced many features but still missed basic editor functionality like a ruler.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: security: Improving security posture across the Microsoft partner ecosystem
item.description:
Microsoft says it is improving security across its partner ecosystem by tightening CSP vetting, applying least-privilege access, increasing monitoring, and promoting risk-management best practices. The post is framed as guidance for partners rather than a product release.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Microsoft named a leader in the Frost Radar for cloud and application runtime security
item.description:
Microsoft says Frost & Sullivan named it a leader in the Frost Radar for cloud and application runtime security. The post frames this as recognition for a unified approach to cloud and application security focused on runtime risk reduction.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Accelerating the quantum-safe timeline
item.description:
Microsoft says it is accelerating quantum-safe readiness and urging organizations to begin transitioning earlier as the perceived risk timeline shifts. The post is a brief announcement pointing readers to guidance on what can be done now.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: dotnet: .NET 8 and .NET 9 will reach End of Support on November 10, 2026
item.description:
Microsoft says .NET 8 and .NET 9 will both reach end of support on November 10, 2026. The post directs users to upgrade to .NET 10, which is the next LTS release.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: Microsoft a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Endpoint Management Platforms
item.description:
Microsoft says it was named a Leader in Forrester Wave: Endpoint Management Platforms, Q2 2026, and reports the highest scores in the current offering and strategy categories.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: StealC and Amadey: Breaking down infostealers and the cybercrime services that deliver them
item.description:
Microsoft’s DCU helped disrupt domains supporting the StealC and Amadey infrastructure on June 24, 2026. The post provides a technical overview of the StealC and Amadey malware families and the cybercrime services used to deliver them.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Guarding AI memory
item.description:
Microsoft Security Blog discusses risks to AI memory and outlines defenses against attacks that target what an AI system remembers. The post is a high-level security overview rather than a product release note.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: AutoJack: How a single page can RCE the host running your AI agent
item.description:
Microsoft describes AutoJack, an exploit chain where a single malicious webpage can cause remote code execution on the host running an AI browsing agent. The attack abuses localhost trust, missing authentication, and unsafe parameter handling in AutoGen Studio’s MCP WebSocket.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: commandline: Introducing the Agentic Resource Discovery specification
item.description:
Microsoft introduced the Agentic Resource Discovery (ARD) specification, an open standard for AI clients to automatically find resources relevant to a task. It was developed with partners including GoDaddy, Google, and Hugging Face.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: dotnet: Join us for .NET Day on Agentic Modernization Livestream
item.description:
Microsoft announced a .NET Day livestream focused on agentic modernization. The post is an event announcement and does not include technical product changes or release details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: ai: Accelerate Edge AI Development with Foundry Local
item.description:
Microsoft announced Foundry Local for edge AI development, aimed at making it easier to build AI experiences that run inside apps, devices, and other edge systems. The post highlights common production challenges such as model packaging, runtime fragmentation, and hardware differences.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: commandline: Introducing Command Line, and the new rules for builders
item.description:
Microsoft introduces a new “rules for builders” framing around agentic AI development, saying the traditional software development lifecycle is no longer sufficient. The post positions the guidance as a shift in workflows and mindset for building with AI agents.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: commandline: Grounding at scale: Engineering the retrieval system for the agentic web
item.description:
Microsoft introduced Web IQ, a new grounding system for the agentic web. The note is brief and does not include implementation details or feature specifics.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: security: Microsoft Build 2026: Securing code, agents, and models across the development lifecycle
item.description:
Microsoft says Build 2026 introduced MDASH and additional security capabilities aimed at securing code, agents, and models across the development lifecycle. The post frames these updates as support for fast AI development with security controls built in.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: commandline: Five ways we’re confusing AI capability and AI reality—and how to bridge the gap
item.description:
Microsoft partnered with Lectures on Tap for Build on Tap, where CTO Kevin Scott discussed a perceived gap between AI capability and how AI is actually being used. The piece frames this as a disconnect between industry expectations and operational reality.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: security: Microsoft is named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Endpoint Protection
item.description:
Microsoft says it was named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Endpoint Protection. The post provides no product changes, feature updates, or technical release details.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: commandline: Inside the Microsoft Agent Framework: How we designed a layered SDK
item.description:
Microsoft describes the Agent Framework as a layered SDK that supports different agent architectures. It highlights agent loops, workflows, and harnesses as the main building blocks for both simple assistants and more complex multi-agent systems.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: commandline: Introducing Microsoft’s EngThrive framework: Understanding developer productivity in the agentic AI era
item.description:
Microsoft introduced EngThrive, a framework for measuring developer productivity in the agentic AI era. It evaluates engineering work across Speed, Ease, Quality, and Thriving.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: commandline: How Excel got agentic: An interview with Microsoft Director of Science Mukul Singh
item.description:
Microsoft says it used two years of research to add agentic capabilities to Excel, based on an interview with Director of Science Mukul Singh. The piece positions this work as part of bringing research into product, with implications beyond Excel.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: security: Microsoft recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Workforce Identity Security Platforms
item.description:
Microsoft says it was named a Leader in The Forrester Wave: Workforce Identity Security Platforms, Q2 2026, with the highest scores in both current offering and strategy. The post does not include product changes or technical release details.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Microsoft Security success stories: How St. Luke’s and ManpowerGroup are securing AI foundations
item.description:
Microsoft highlights how St. Luke’s and ManpowerGroup are using governance, identity, and cloud security controls to support AI adoption at scale. The post frames security as part of the foundation for AI deployment rather than a separate concern.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Mini Shai Hulud: Compromised @antv npm packages enable CI/CD credential theft
item.description:
Microsoft reports that compromised @antv npm packages were used to deploy the Mini Shai-Hulud payload, which runs during npm install and steals CI/CD secrets from Linux-based automation environments. The malware targets credentials from GitHub, AWS, Kubernetes, Vault, npm, and 1Password.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Securing the gaming culture of cultures
item.description:
The post says gaming platforms and communities have unique security challenges and discusses ways to better protect them. It is a Microsoft Security Blog article focused on securing gaming culture and platforms.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Introducing RAMPART and Clarity: Open source tools to bring safety into Agent development workflow
item.description:
Microsoft announced two open-source tools, RAMPART and Clarity, aimed at adding safety checks to agent development workflows. The post frames the need around enterprise AI agents that can access data, execute code, and take actions across connected systems.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Exposing Fox Tempest: A malware-signing service operation
item.description:
Microsoft says Fox Tempest is a financially motivated threat actor running a malware-signing-as-a-service operation used by other cybercriminals to help distribute malicious code, including ransomware. The service has been linked to groups such as Vanilla Tempest and Storm.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Defense at AI speed: Microsoft’s new multi-model agentic security system tops leading industry benchmark
item.description:
Microsoft announced MDASH, a new multi-model agentic scanning harness for AI-powered cyber defense. The post says it topped a leading industry benchmark, but provides no technical details in the excerpt.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Defending consumer web properties against modern DDoS attacks
item.description:
Microsoft published guidance on defending consumer web properties against modern DDoS attacks, emphasizing layered security, resilient architecture, and graceful service degradation.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: When prompts become shells: RCE vulnerabilities in AI agent frameworks
item.description:
Microsoft says new research found prompt injection issues in AI agent frameworks can lead to remote code execution. The post outlines affected areas and recommends securing agents against these flaws.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: World Passkey Day: Advancing passwordless authentication
item.description:
Microsoft says it is advancing passkey adoption as a replacement for passwords, with the goals of reducing phishing risk and simplifying sign-in. The post is framed around World Passkey Day and passwordless authentication.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: ​​Microsoft named an overall leader in KuppingerCole Analyst’s 2026 Emerging AI Security Operations Center (SOC) report ​​
item.description:
Microsoft says KuppingerCole Analyst named it an Overall Leader and Market Leader in the 2026 Emerging AI Security Operations Center (SOC) report. The post frames automation and AI as central to future cybersecurity.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: ClickFix campaign uses fake macOS utilities lures to deliver infostealers
item.description:
Microsoft reports a ClickFix campaign targeting macOS users with fake utility fixes that prompt victims to run malicious Terminal commands. The malware is used to steal credentials, crypto wallets, and other sensitive data while bypassing traditional defenses.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Microsoft Agent 365, now generally available, expands capabilities and integrations
item.description:
Microsoft announced general availability of Agent 365 and previewed new features for discovering and managing shadow AI agents, including local agents such as OpenClaw and Claude Code.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog
